action.hypothesis: The gated K=128 EF=16 model appears sensitive to optimization, and the worse full result at LR=3.2e-3 suggests overshooting; reducing the signum learning rate to 8e-4 may improve full-run reconstruction by trading some proxy speed for a stabler optimization trajectory.
action.summary: Keep the gated K=128 EF=16 architecture fixed and continue the gated stabilization sweep by testing a lower learning rate of 8e-4 in proxy.
action.self_review: This follows directly from the failed higher-LR full promotion and changes one generic, launcher-safe parameter while preserving the current best architecture. The main risk is under-updating the larger gated model, but LR is now the clearest remaining stabilization axis after 3.2e-3 regressed.
notes_to_memory: Gated EF=16 remains the best full frontier, but LR=3.2e-3 improved proxy while degrading full quality, indicating optimization sensitivity rather than a settled LR optimum.
This round tests a lower LR on the same gated EF=16 anchor to see whether a more conservative optimization path yields better full-run generalization.
The search remains focused on beating the K=128 gated baseline materially before considering smaller K or cost reductions.
next_hypotheses: If LR=8e-4 improves proxy FVU, promote to full and compare directly against the current gated K=128 frontier.
If LR=8e-4 is also worse, test LR=4e-4 as the last conservative LR bracket on gated EF=16.
If lower-LR sweeps plateau, shift to gated-specific regularization or architecture refinements instead of further generic optimizer tuning.
